blockers_fixed.root_cause: int(mask.sum()) CPU sync + data-dependent 'if n_valid==0:' branch -> UserError: Could not guard on data-dependent expression Eq(u0, 0)
blockers_fixed.fix: tensor accumulation for n_valid, torch.where replaces data-dependent branch
inspect.unwrap(backbone.forward.__func__) strips decorator wrapper; LlamaModel.forward handles use_cache=None via self.config.use_cache internally

remaining_limitation.issue: Tensor.backward() inside fullgraph=True is Unsupported in torch 2.6
remaining_limitation.effect: compile scope limited to forward-only; loss.backward() must remain outside compiled region

assessment.gap: 1.5x target NOT achievable from torch.compile in torch 2.6 on h2048-d12
assessment.root_cause_of_low_speedup: RTX 4090 + torch 2.6 + transformers 5.2 already uses FlashAttention + cuBLAS optimal kernels; inductor kernel fusion has minimal room on large matmul-dominated LLM forward passes
assessment.mode_reduce_overhead_result: OOM at batch=8 (CUDA graph recording overhead exhausts VRAM)
assessment.implication: compile lever contributes ~2%, not the 1.5x assumed in fp-42 two-lever plan; §3 gate plan needs revision

eli_integration_notes: Apply both patch functions before torch.compile. Compile fwd_fn (forward-only, no backward in compiled region). In _bench_cell: extract fwd portion into separate closure, compile that, call loss.backward() + opts.step() outside the compiled region. The bench loop timing is unaffected (t_opt measured outside as before).
probes: c04_compile_probe9.py (job df6f4a22): fwd-only compile batch=8 + reduce-overhead
